programmes.show_description: Arthur is convincing Alf to get married to Mrs Hollingbery. Alf is toying with the idea, not for love, but for the opportunity to put pensions together and get the house cheap off the council! S4 E2
With Boomtown Rats. The legendary BAFTA-winning comedy extravaganza from Thames TV (1980). All in the best possible taste!
Simon undergoes considerable discomfort to bring a beautiful but spoiled girl to her senses - and takes her on a hundred-mile trip. S1 E10: The Golden Journey
A selection of live performances from TV music shows in the 1960s for a genuine taste of the best of the '60s!

Your favourite music from the 1960s - non-stop.
A famed US general was injured in a car crash in Germany, appeared to recover, then died suddenly. Accident, murder, or post war tension? Narrated by Robert Powell.
We tell the stories of some of America's fighting men in the Vietnam War who persevered and triumphed over hazards, ranging from river patrols to the air crews who delivered weapons on targets.
Two decaying Second World War wrecks, the SS Skytteren and SS Richard Montgomery, are investigated as their dormant cargoes now pose a deadly threat. Ep9: Toxic Wrecks
There are some news events that change the world. In this episode, we look at the events of September 11th 2001.
A look into the lives of the rich and famous - this time we explore the rise of a muscle-bound Austrian from the body-building circuit to becoming Governor of California: Arnold Schwarzenegger.
The Republic P-47 Thunderbolt is a WWII-era fighter aircraft. It was a successful high-altitude fighter, and it also served as the foremost American fighter-bomber in the ground-attack role. S3 E3
The brave men and women who fought along the tides of the Pacific share their stories of Japanese and US forces facing off with an intense determination and will to survive. Ep8: Hell In The Pacific
We compare the elite teams at the frontlines of the fight against terror against past battles comparing American riflemen with British line soldiers and the story of American sniper Timothy Murphy.
RAF Coastal Command is perhaps the lesser known of Britain's wartime RAF Commands however the tasks faced by Coastal Command were gigantic, ranging from submarine hunting to convoy protection.
Examining the history of war tactics. A raid is an offensive military operation that aims to achieve a specific objective. It is a battleplan which often requires elite special forces. S2 E2
The story of the men that cracked the Nazis' Enigma code, from Polish mathematicians to the establishment at Bletchley Park and the work of Alan Turing. Ep4: The Men Who Cracked Enigma
Rockets had a huge impact on World War II - hand-held rocket launchers such as the American bazooka and German panzerfaust enabled infantry to tackle tanks head-on. Ep8: Rockets
They are the means by which to confuse an enemy - from inflatable decoy tanks to fake radio broadcasts, deception played a key role in the course of WWII. S1 E9: Tools of Deception
When American forces began the reconquest of the Philippine islands in 1944, they faced a terrifying weapon - the Kamikaze suicide attack. Ep9: The Kamikazes

Britain faced Nazi Germany in July 1940 as Winston Churchill set up the Commandos, a fighting force to raid enemy coasts, and ordered a major assault on Vaagso in Norway. Ep6: Arctic Commando Assaults
Jack is injured after demolishing Mandrake Place - a beautiful country house owned by Sir Horatio Manners. Jessie offers to give Jack and alibi but he refuses. S3 E1: A House Divided
Things start to go wrong for Arthur after he sells an over priced and unroadworthy Austin Allegro to a nun. S10 E5: The Immaculate Contraption
While George makes political manoeuvres at home, Ross sails to France to rescue Dwight from the French Revolution. Starring Aidan Turner. S3 E5
Drama series following the exploits of Life on Mars DCI Gene Hunt. Hunt and his faithful sidekicks are drawn to the London Met where he turns his attentions to taking on London's criminals. S1 E3
Alf and Mrs Hollingbery are now engaged to be married and he has already started to enjoy her cooking. They treat Arthur, who thinks he's won the pools, to a celebration meal... S4 E3
With Suzi Quatro and Joe Jackson. The legendary BAFTA-winning comedy extravaganza from Thames TV (1980). All in the best possible taste!
In Rome, Simon cleans up a charity racket in his inimitable style, and paves the way to a new life for a gang of juvenile delinquents. S1 E12: The Charitable Countess

Your favourite music from the 1960s - non-stop.
By December 1941, did Britain know of the Pearl Harbour plans, and did Churchill warn Roosevelt so he could move the US carriers. Narrated by Robert Powell.

Experts piece together evidence to reveal a giant ring of prehistoric shafts, 20 times the size of Stonehenge and created over 4,000 years ago that has been completely hidden from sight until now. Ep1
Two research teams are granted special permission to investigate what happened to the WWI warship HMS Hampshire and HMS Vanguard, which was commissioned after WWII. Ep10: Scotland's Sea Secrets
...Were You? There are some news events that change the world - in this episode, we take you back to Sunday the 11th of February, 1990 and the release of Nelson Mandela.
From humble beginnings, Barack Obama rose to become the most powerful man in the world - we take a look at his life.
The Yakovlev Yak-1 was a Soviet fighter aircraft of World War II - it was a single-seat monoplane with a composite structure and wooden wings and was a fast and competitive fighter aircraft. S3 E4
The war reaches a critical turning point as Hitler expands it to a second front where German soldiers endure the unrelenting climate of Russia. Ep9: Eastern Front
Our exploration of the elite teams that have led the USA's battle against terror continues with a look at the US Special Forces and the founding of the legendary Green Berets and Navy SEALs.
Examining the history of war tactics. Strategic bombing takes war to the heartland of the enemy. It seeks to destroy not only an enemy's ability to wage war, but also its will to fight on. S2 E3
The series begins before the First World War when military commanders struggled to understand what uses aviation might have. Ep1: Useless For The Purposes Of War
The story of Nazi heavy cruisers and battleships during WWII including the Bismarck, how she got into the Atlantic, and the steps taken to defeat her. Ep5: The Men Who Sank The Bismarck
The machine gun did not dominate the battlefield of WWII - unlike WWI - but it remained a highly potent weapon including being incorporated into the wings of fighter planes. Ep9: The Machine Gun
The 'Desert Fox' Field Marshal Rommel seemed to have a sixth sense when it came to Allied plans and positions - we take a look into how Rommel's winning streak was broken. S1 E10: Rommel's Enigma
The Royal Navy fought a long campaign against the German U-boats and surface ships in the Atlantic; its Pacific Fleet helped win victory over Japan. Ep10: The Royal Navy
Thugs beat Jack up; he knows which 'posh fellas' sent them and plans his revenge as Jessie's husband has a heart-to-heart with Jack and clears the air. S3 E2: A Tiger, A Lamb & A Basket... of Fruit
Arthur's venture into corporate hospitality takes him and Ray into company that is both glamorous and dangerous. S10 E6: All Quiet on the West End Front
While George makes political manoeuvres at home, Ross sails to France to rescue Dwight from the French Revolution. Starring Aidan Turner. S3 E5
Drama series following the exploits of Life on Mars's DCI Gene Hunt. Alex smells a conspiracy when the body of a worker at a nuclear research centre disappears from the morgue. S1 E4
